info.title: Datafiniti API
info.description:
The Datafiniti API v3 provides developers with access to structured data for Businesses, Products, and Properties. V3 uses GET requests with query parameters and API key-based authentication in the URL. Note: V3 is a legacy version. Datafiniti recommends migrating to V4 which uses POST requests, Bearer token authentication, and consolidated download endpoints.

Key differences from V4:
- All data endpoints use GET instead of POST
- Authentication is via API key in the URL (Basic Auth) rather than Bearer token header
- A separate /results endpoint is required to fetch download file URLs (removed in V4)
- No People data endpoint (added in V4)
- No Views management endpoints (added in V4)
- No Real-time data endpoints (added in V4)

tags.name: Data
tags.description: Endpoints for searching and downloading business, product, and property data

tags.name: Requests
tags.description: Endpoint for monitoring the status of download requests

tags.name: Results
tags.description: Endpoint for retrieving download file URLs

tags.name: Account
tags.description: Endpoint for viewing your account information

get.description:
The data endpoint lets you run preview searches and initiate downloads for business data.

If you set `download=false` (or don't set it), the API call will return a list of matching businesses for your query (up to 10 records). If you set `download=true`, it will return a request object that can be used to monitor the status of the download via the `/requests` endpoint.

200.description: Successful search response (preview mode) or download request initiated

303.description: Redirect to the /requests endpoint to monitor download status (when download=true)

Location.description: URL of the request status endpoint

value.error: field_name does not exist in business schema

get.description:
The data endpoint lets you run preview searches and initiate downloads for product data.

If you set `download=false` (or don't set it), the API call will return a list of matching products for your query. If you don't set `records` when `download=false`, it will return up to 10 products. You can set `records` to a value between 0 and 10.

If you set `download=true`, it will return a request object that can be used to monitor the status of the download request. If you don't set `records` when `download=true`, it will initiate a download request for all matching products. You can set `records` to a value between 1 and the number of matching products.

200.description: Successful search response (preview mode) or download request initiated

303.description: Redirect to the /requests endpoint to monitor download status (when download=true)

400.description: Bad request - invalid field name, query syntax, or view name

value.error: field_name does not exist in product schema

get.description:
The data endpoint lets you run preview searches and initiate downloads for property data.

If you set `download=false` (or don't set it), the API call will return a list of matching properties for your query. If you don't set `records` when `download=false`, it will return up to 10 properties. You can set `records` to a value between 0 and 10.

If you set `download=true`, it will return a request object that can be used to monitor the status of the download request. If you don't set `records` when `download=true`, it will initiate a download request for all matching records. You can set `records` to a value between 1 and the number of matching records.

200.description: Successful search response (preview mode) or download request initiated

303.description: Redirect to the /requests endpoint to monitor download status (when download=true)

400.description: Bad request - invalid field name, query syntax, or view name

value.error: field_name does not exist in property schema

get.description: The requests endpoint lets you monitor the status of all download requests you have initiated. Returns an array of request objects with status, progress, and metadata about each download.

Monitor the status of a specific download request by its ID. Check if the download is still STARTED or has COMPLETED. Once completed, use the `/results/{request_id}` endpoint to get the download file URLs.

parameters.name: request_id
parameters.description: The unique ID of the download request (returned when you initiate a download)

400.description: Invalid request ID. You may be using an incorrect ID. Call /requests/ without an ID to see all your requests.

get.description:
The results endpoint lets you view the URLs of available data files for your completed download. Each result object contains a URL where you can download the data file. Result files expire 7 days after creation.

Note: This endpoint was removed in V4. In V4, result file URLs are included directly in the download response.

parameters.name: request_id
parameters.description: The unique ID of the download request whose results you want to retrieve

200.description: List of result file objects with download URLs

404.description: Results don't exist. If your download request status still says STARTED, wait until it says COMPLETED. You may also be using the wrong request ID.

500.description: You may be trying to look at an invalid request ID.

get.description: The users endpoint lets you view the status of your Datafiniti account, including your plan information, credit usage, and account details.

ApiKeyAuth.description: V3 uses Basic Authentication with your API token as the username and no password. In URL form: https://YOUR_API_TOKEN:@api.datafiniti.co/v3/... In cURL: --user YOUR_API_TOKEN: (note the trailing colon). Get your token from the Datafiniti Web Portal at https://portal.datafiniti.co.

query.name: q
query.description: Search query using Datafiniti query syntax. Supports boolean operators (AND, OR), exact match with quotes, wildcards (*), negation (-), range queries, and nested field queries. Example: `categories:hotels AND country:US`

records.name: records
records.description: Number of records to return. For preview (download=false): 0-10, defaults to 10. For download (download=true): 1 to total matching records. If omitted during download, all matching records will be downloaded.

format.name: format
format.description: Output format for the data. JSON returns data as JSON objects. CSV returns data in CSV format.

download.name: download
download.description: Set to true to initiate a bulk download. When false (default), returns up to 10 preview records immediately. When true, returns a 303 redirect to the /requests endpoint to monitor download progress.

view.name: view
view.description: The view determines which fields are returned in the response and the order they appear. Different views are available for each data type. Examples for businesses: businesses_all, businesses_all_nested. Examples for products: products_all, products_basicFields, products_pricesFlat. Examples for properties: properties_all, properties_all_nested.

SearchResponse.description: Response returned when download=false (preview mode). Contains matching records inline.

num_found.description: Total number of records in Datafiniti matching the query

total_cost.description: Number of credits consumed by this request

records.description: Array of matching records. The fields present depend on the data type and view used.

DownloadRequestResponse.description: Response returned when download=true. Contains a request object array for monitoring the download.

RequestObject.description: Represents the status of a download request. Poll this object via /requests/{id} until status changes from STARTED to COMPLETED.

id.description: Unique identifier for the download request

query.description: The query that was used for this download

dataFormat.description: The format of the download files

accountId.description: Your API token used for this request

planId.description: The Datafiniti plan level you're using

view.description: The view used for this download, determining which fields are included

async.description: Should always be 1. Indicates this is a download (async) request.

status.description: The current progress of the request. STARTED while running, COMPLETED when finished.

error.description: An error message if anything went wrong with the download. Empty string if no errors.

numDownloaded.description: Number of records that have been downloaded so far. Increases as the download progresses.

numFound.description: Total number of records in Datafiniti that matched your query

numRequested.description: Number of records you requested to download. If you didn't set records, this equals numFound.

date_started.description: Date and time the download started

date_completed.description: Date and time the download completed. May match date_started if still in progress.

ResultObject.description: Represents a downloadable result file from a completed download request. Result files expire 7 days after creation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the result object

url.description: The URL of the data file. Fetch the content of this URL to download the file. This is typically an S3 pre-signed URL.

request_id.description: A unique identifier for your download request

downloaded.description: An internal field. You can ignore this.

imported.description: An internal field. You can ignore this.

date_created.description: Date the result file was created. The result file will expire 7 days after this date.

plan_id.description: The Datafiniti plan to which you're subscribed

type.description: Your account type. Should be set to 'user'.

active.description: Whether or not your account is active

available_downloads.description: The number of records you can download at this time

date_registered.description: The date your account was created

error.description: Error message describing what went wrong

Unauthorized.description: You're using an invalid API token. Check your account page on the Datafiniti Portal (https://portal.datafiniti.co) to make sure you have the right token.
